name: network-firewall
title: AWS Firewall — AWS Network Firewall
summary: Creates an AWS Network Firewall with stateless and stateful (Suricata-compatible, STRICT_ORDER) rule groups, a firewall policy, endpoint placement across dedicated subnets, optional CloudWatch logging, and deletion protection on by default.

governance.name: prefix
governance.description: Short organization or project prefix. First token of every resource name: {prefix}-{cloud}-{environment}-{resource-type}-{suffix}.

governance.name: environment
governance.description: Deployment environment identifier (e.g., dev, test, prod). Used in resource names and mandatory tags.

governance.name: owner
governance.description: Team or individual that owns the deployed resources. Applied as the mandatory 'owner' tag.

governance.name: cost_center
governance.description: Cost center code for chargeback/showback. Applied as the mandatory 'cost_center' tag.

governance.name: additional_tags
governance.description: Optional additional tags merged beneath the mandatory tag set. Mandatory tags win on key collision.

governance.name: name_suffix
governance.description: Final token of every resource name, used to disambiguate multiple instances of this module.

module.name: vpc_id
module.description: ID of the VPC in which the firewall endpoints are created. Supplied by ID — no framework dependency is implied.

module.name: firewall_subnet_ids
module.description: Subnet IDs (one per AZ recommended) dedicated to AWS Network Firewall endpoints. Must not host other workloads.

module.name: stateless_rule_groups
module.type: map(object({ capacity = number priority = number rules = map(object({ priority = number actions = list(string) source_cidrs = optional(list(string), ["0.0.0.0/0"]) destination_cidrs = optional(list(string), ["0.0.0.0/0"]) protocols = optional(list(number), []) source_port_from = optional(number) source_port_to = optional(number) destination_port_from = optional(number) destination_port_to = optional(number) })) }))
module.description: Stateless rule groups keyed by logical name. capacity is the AWS rule-group capacity reservation; priority orders groups inside the policy.

module.name: stateful_rule_groups
module.type: map(object({ capacity = number priority = number rules_string = string }))
module.description: Stateful rule groups keyed by logical name, expressed as Suricata-compatible rules_string. Policy uses STRICT_ORDER evaluation; priority orders groups.

module.name: stateless_default_actions
module.description: Default actions for full packets that match no stateless rule (e.g., [\"aws:forward_to_sfe\"] to hand off to the stateful engine).

module.name: stateless_fragment_default_actions
module.description: Default actions for fragmented packets that match no stateless rule.

module.name: stateful_default_actions
module.description: Default actions for the stateful engine under STRICT_ORDER (e.g., [\"aws:drop_strict\"] for default-deny, [\"aws:alert_established\"] for monitor mode).

module.name: delete_protection
module.description: Enable deletion protection on the firewall (governance/resource-locks). Must be set false and applied before the firewall can be destroyed.

module.name: subnet_change_protection
module.description: Prevent endpoint subnet changes on the firewall.

module.name: policy_change_protection
module.description: Prevent the firewall policy association from being changed.

module.name: enable_logging
module.description: Whether to send ALERT and FLOW logs to module-managed CloudWatch log groups.

module.name: log_retention_days
module.description: Retention period in days for firewall CloudWatch log groups.

outputs.name: firewall_ids
outputs.description: Map with single key 'firewall' to the AWS Network Firewall ARN (the provider uses ARN as ID).

outputs.name: rule_ids
outputs.description: Map of 'stateless|stateful/<group-key>' to rule group ARN.

outputs.name: endpoint_ids
outputs.description: Map of availability zone to firewall VPC endpoint ID. Use these as route targets to steer traffic through the firewall.

outputs.name: log_group_names
outputs.description: Names of the ALERT and FLOW CloudWatch log groups, or empty map when logging is disabled.
